title: Lumen GM Hub — Build Plan (v0.1–v1.5+)
content:
# Lumen GM Hub — plan.md
> Last updated: 2026-03-12
> Full spec: `projects/lumen/spec_v3.pdf` (18 pages — reference doc, not for daily reading)

## What Is Lumen?

AI-powered companion app for PF2e games run in Foundry VTT (v13). Captures session audio via a **built-in VoIP client**, transcribes in real time with speaker attribution, generates GM-facing suggestions, maintains campaign memory, detects continuity conflicts, and writes session notes to Foundry.

## Current Phase: v0.1 — Pipeline + Foundry Connector + Transcript Viewer

**PoC completed 2026-03-11 with Go verdict.** The audio pipeline is validated. We are now building the first product milestone.

**v0.1 definition of "done":** A GM can start a session in Lumen, have players join via browser, see a live speaker-attributed transcript in the Hub, and see Foundry game state (scene, party, combat) reflected in the Hub UI. The transcript viewer is clean and usable — not a debug tool.

---

## Architecture (VoIP-First — Validated)

```
Player A ──WebRTC──→                              ──→ VAD → Whisper → [Player A]: "text"
Player B ──WebRTC──→   GM's Machine (Lumen Hub)   ──→ VAD → Whisper → [Player B]: "text"
Player C ──WebRTC──→                              ──→ VAD → Whisper → [Player C]: "text"
GM Mic   ──local───→                              ──→ VAD → Whisper → [GM]: "text"
                                                            │
                                                  Chronological merge → Attributed transcript
                                                            │
                              Foundry VTT ◄──WebSocket──► Lumen Hub (Electron)
                              (scene, combat,                │
                               party, actors)          Transcript Viewer UI
```

**Key architectural decisions (see `brain/Lumen/lumen-architecture.md` for full ADRs):**

- **VoIP-first, not Discord loopback.** Per-stream capture = deterministic speaker attribution. (ADR-001)
- **No pyannote, no VB-Cable, no WASAPI.** Eliminated by VoIP architecture. (ADR-001, ADR-002)
- **Electron for the Hub.** Bundles Chromium's WebRTC stack. (ADR-003)
- **`large-v3` Whisper model as default.** Confirmed on 8GB cards alongside Foundry. (ADR-006)
- **Single shared Whisper instance with segment queue.** CTranslate2 shares weights. (ADR-007)
- **Pipeline stability validated.** 60 min, 16,548 segments, 0 errors, no VRAM leak. (ADR-008)

## Tech Stack

| Component | Technology | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Transcription | `faster-whisper` (CTranslate2) | Local GPU, Python, `large-v3` default |
| Voice Activity Detection | Silero VAD | Per-stream, tuned (threshold 0.65, min 300ms, RMS gate 0.01) |
| VoIP (GM side) | Electron + WebRTC | Built into Hub |
| VoIP (Player side) | Browser HTML/JS + WebRTC | No install |
| Signaling | WebSocket server | Localhost for v0.1, cloud for production |
| Audio codec | Opus (48kHz, 64kbps default) | Configurable bitrate |
| Hub application | Electron | Desktop app |
| Foundry module | JavaScript or TypeScript | Evaluate as first task (see Step 0) |
| Hub UI framework | TBD | Evaluate as first task — must support clean, usable UI |

## VRAM Budget (RTX 3070 — 8GB, Validated)

| Whisper Model | VRAM Peak | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `large-v3` | ~5,851 MB (single), ~6,349 MB (3-stream sustained) | **Recommended default** |
| `medium` | ~3,899 MB | Fallback for constrained systems |

Total pipeline under real workload (Foundry + Hub + 3 streams): ~6.3 GB avg, ~2.3 GB headroom on 8GB card.

---

## v0.1 Build Plan

### Step 0 — Architecture Decisions & Code Strategy (Day 1)

Before writing any v0.1 code, evaluate and decide:

**0a. PoC code quality assessment.** Review the existing PoC codebase (`poc/shared/`, `poc/phase2/`, `poc/phase3/`). Decide whether to restructure the validated modules into a clean `src/` layout or do a clean rewrite referencing the PoC. Document the decision and rationale.

Factors to consider:
- Are the core modules (whisper_engine.py, vad.py, config.py) production-ready or PoC-quality?
- Is the Electron scaffolding from Phase 2 reusable or too hacky?
- Is the WebRTC signaling server worth carrying forward?
- How much technical debt exists in the PoC code?

**0b. Foundry module language.** Evaluate JavaScript vs TypeScript for the Foundry v13 module. Standard Foundry modules are JS, but TS offers type safety. Consider what the Foundry module ecosystem prefers and what will cause the least friction.

**0c. Hub UI framework.** The transcript viewer needs to be clean and usable — not a debug tool. Evaluate what UI approach works best within Electron (React, vanilla HTML/CSS, Svelte, etc.). The PoC used inline HTML — that won't scale for a real UI. Consider what Cole can maintain and iterate on.

**Output:** Written decisions for all three questions, added as ADRs to `brain/Lumen/lumen-architecture.md`. Project structure scaffolded based on decisions.

---

### Step 1 — Project Restructure (2–3 days)

Based on Step 0 decisions, set up the production project structure. Example layout (adapt based on decisions):

```
lumen/
├── src/
│   ├── pipeline/              ← audio pipeline (Python)
│   │   ├── whisper_engine.py  ← faster-whisper wrapper
│   │   ├── vad.py             ← Silero VAD wrapper
│   │   ├── transcription_server.py  ← WebSocket server receiving audio, producing transcript
│   │   ├── config.py          ← model config, VAD params
│   │   └── requirements.txt
│   ├── hub/                   ← Electron application
│   │   ├── main/              ← Electron main process
│   │   ├── renderer/          ← UI (framework TBD)
│   │   ├── preload/           ← contextBridge IPC
│   │   └── package.json
│   ├── signaling/             ← WebSocket signaling server
│   │   └── server.py (or .js)
│   ├── player-client/         ← browser voice client
│   │   └── index.html         ← single-file, zero-dependency
│   └── foundry-module/        ← Foundry VTT v13 module
│       ├── module.json        ← Foundry module manifest
│       ├── scripts/           ← module JS/TS
│       └── templates/         ← Handlebars templates (if needed)
├── poc/                       ← archived PoC code (reference only)
├── benchmarks/                ← carried forward from PoC
├── docs/
│   └── spec_v3.pdf
├── .gitignore
├── README.md
└── package.json (or monorepo config)
```

Acceptance criteria:
- Clean separation between pipeline (Python), Hub (Electron/JS), Foundry module, and player client
- PoC code archived in `poc/` — not deleted, but not imported into `src/`
- Core pipeline modules validated: can load Whisper, capture mic audio, transcribe, and output to terminal from the new structure
- All dependencies documented and pinned

---

### Step 2 — Foundry VTT Module: Read-Only Connector (1–2 weeks)

Build the Foundry v13 module that connects to the Lumen Hub.

**What the module does (v0.1):**
- Registers as a Foundry v13 module with a "Lumen Hub" panel in the sidebar
- Connects to the Hub via WebSocket on `ws://127.0.0.1:17172` (per spec Section 20)
- Pairing flow: Hub displays a pairing code, GM enters it in Foundry panel once, token stored in world settings
- Streams game state to the Hub on change:
  - Active scene (name, ID, thumbnail)
  - Combat state (active encounter, initiative order, current turn)
  - Party roster (actor names, player names, HP, conditions)
  - Actor updates (HP changes, condition add/remove during combat)
- Receives live transcript from Hub and displays in a read-only panel within Foundry (optional — may defer to v0.2)

**What the module does NOT do (v0.1):**
- No write-back to Foundry (no journal writes, no HP/condition changes from Hub)
- No Campaign Ledger creation
- No player-facing read-only snapshot
- No compendium queries (that's for the suggestion engine in v0.2)

**Key references:**
- Spec Section 3.4 (Foundry module component)
- Spec Section 5.1 (pairing flow)
- Spec Section 20 (WebSocket API contract)

Acceptance criteria:
- Module installs in Foundry v13 via manifest URL or local folder
- Successful WebSocket connection to Hub with pairing flow
- Hub receives scene, combat, and party data in real time as GM navigates Foundry
- Connection survives scene changes and combat start/stop without dropping

---

### Step 3 — Hub Transcript Viewer UI (1–2 weeks)

Rebuild the Electron Hub UI from the PoC debug page into a clean, usable interface.

**Panels (visible simultaneously during session):**

1. **Connection Status** — signaling server status, Foundry pairing status, VoIP session state
2. **Connected Players** — list of connected players with speaking indicators and connection quality
3. **Foundry State** — active scene name, combat status (round/turn), party roster summary. Proves the Foundry module is feeding data to the Hub
4. **Live Transcript** — scrolling real-time transcript with:
   - Speaker name (color-coded per player)
   - Timestamp
   - Transcription text
   - Latency indicator (subtle, for debugging — not prominent)
   - Auto-scroll with manual scroll lock (if GM scrolls up, don't snap back)
5. **Session Controls** — Start/Stop session, session duration timer, session link for sharing with players

**Design principles:**
- Dark theme (GMs play in dim rooms)
- High contrast text for readability during sessions
- Compact enough for a secondary monitor or a side panel alongside Foundry
- No visual clutter — every element earns its screen space
- Keyboard accessible (tab between panels, hotkeys for session start/stop)

**What the UI does NOT include (v0.1):**
- No suggestion queue or triage actions (v0.2)
- No conflict alerts (v0.3)
- No campaign memory browser (v0.3)
- No post-session report (v0.5)
- No settings/preferences panel beyond basic session controls

Acceptance criteria:
- GM can start a session, see players connect, see Foundry state update, and read a live transcript — all from one window
- UI is visually clean and usable for a 3–4 hour session without eye strain
- Transcript auto-scrolls during active play but doesn't fight the GM if they scroll up to re-read something
- Works on both primary and secondary monitors (responsive to different window sizes)

---

### Step 4 — Integration & Session Testing (1 week)

Wire everything together and run a real integrated test.

**Integration targets:**
- VoIP voice connection (Hub + browser player clients)
- Per-stream transcription pipeline (VAD → Whisper → labeled transcript)
- Foundry module streaming game state to Hub
- Hub UI displaying transcript + Foundry state simultaneously
- Session logging to local file (JSONL + readable text)

**Test scenarios:**
- Solo test: GM + 1 browser player tab, Foundry running with an active scene and mock combat
- Simulated multi-player: pipe test audio through multiple streams (reuse Phase 3 stability harness)
- Scene change: switch scenes in Foundry, verify Hub UI updates
- Combat: start encounter in Foundry, advance initiative, verify Hub reflects combat state
- Session lifecycle: start session → play for 15+ minutes → stop session → verify log file written

**Stability validation:**
- Run the simulated multi-player test for 30+ minutes
- Monitor VRAM and latency (reuse Phase 3 monitoring approach)
- Confirm no regressions from PoC benchmarks

Acceptance criteria:
- All integration targets working in a single session
- No crashes or degradation over 30+ minutes
- Session log file contains clean, speaker-attributed transcript with Foundry context events (scene changes, combat rounds)
- v0.1 milestone is complete

---

## v0.1 Summary

| Step | Work | Duration | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|
| 0 | Architecture decisions + code strategy | 1 day | None |
| 1 | Project restructure | 2–3 days | Step 0 |
| 2 | Foundry VTT module (read-only) | 1–2 weeks | Step 1 |
| 3 | Hub transcript viewer UI | 1–2 weeks | Step 1 (can parallel with Step 2) |
| 4 | Integration & session testing | 1 week | Steps 2 + 3 |

**Estimated total: 4–6 weeks** part-time alongside Sylvara.

Steps 2 and 3 can be parallelized — the Foundry module and the Hub UI are independent until integration in Step 4. In practice, Code will likely alternate between them.

---

## Post-v0.1 Roadmap

| Milestone | Scope | Key Features |
|---|---|---|
| **v0.2** | Suggestion engine | BYO API keys (OpenAI/Anthropic), suggestion categories, control panel, triage actions, Foundry compendium queries for rules reminders |
| **v0.3** | Campaign memory + conflict detection | Memory population methods, Ledger structure, conflict types, interrupt thresholds, resolution flow |
| **v0.4** | Campaign Setup Wizard + note import | Wizard steps (identity, tone, table setup, lines & veils, note import), Obsidian/World Anvil/PDF/docx support |
| **v0.5** | Post-session mode | Written summary, NPC/faction deltas, unresolved threads, prep suggestions, GM feedback |
| **v0.6** | Write permissions | Journal append, actor HP delta, condition add/remove — all GM-approved, never automatic |
| **v0.7** | Player-facing features | Read-only Campaign Ledger in Foundry, section visibility controls |
| **v1.0** | Production launch (local tier) | Subscription/licensing, onboarding tutorial, sample campaign, TURN relay, production signaling server |
| **v1.1** | Starfinder 2e support | SF2e system module compendiums, expanded rules data (see `brain/Lumen/lumen.md` Planned Expansions) |
| **v1.5+** | Cloud tier | Premium subscription, cloud media server (LiveKit), GPU Whisper instances, no-GPU-required tier |

---

## Business Model Summary

Two-tier subscription:

| Tier | Audio Path | Transcription | GPU Required? | Price |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Local | P2P to GM's machine | Local Whisper | Yes (6+ GB VRAM) | Free or $3–5/mo |
| Cloud | To Lumen media server | Cloud Whisper (large-v3) | No | $12–20/mo |

Both tiers share the same Hub, suggestions, memory, Foundry integration. Players don't know which tier the GM uses.

See `brain/Lumen/lumen-business.md` for detailed cost modeling and pricing rationale.

---

## PoC Results Archive

Full PoC documentation preserved for reference:

- `benchmarks/phase1_vram.md` — Whisper VRAM/latency benchmarks across all model sizes
- `benchmarks/phase3_stability.md` — 60-minute stability test results
- `poc/` — archived PoC source code
- ADRs 001–008 in `brain/Lumen/lumen-architecture.md`

---

---
*This is an operational document. Keep it updated as steps complete. Major changes require the two-AI review loop.*
